one_line_thesis: Multi-state regulated utility compounding rate base through a $60B 2026-2030 capex plan; watch at ~$80 (7.84% synthesis IRR) until price dislocation or clearer wildfire recovery.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Xcel Energy is a multi-state regulated utility investing heavily in grid modernization, renewables, and transmission across Minnesota, Colorado, Texas, New Mexico, and Wisconsin. FY2025 ongoing diluted earnings were $3.80 per share (GAAP $3.42 after the Marshall wildfire charge); Q1 2026 diluted earnings were $0.89, up from $0.84 a year earlier. At $79.84 per share, Marvin's base case implies about 7.84% per year over seven years on normalized regulated earnings power, below our ~15% target. The moat is narrow (geographic monopoly plus regulation), and dhando is partial: allowed-return fram...

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: NSP-Minnesota signed electric service agreement to power a new Google data center; customer pays all incremental costs under Minnesota large-load rules.
Published $60B base capital plan for 2026-2030 ($11.6B in 2025 actual); funding mix ~50% cash from operations, ~38% debt, ~12% equity.
FY2025 dividends declared $2.28 per share (+4%); ongoing diluted EPS $3.80 vs GAAP $3.42.
PSCo recognized $287M Marshall wildfire litigation settlement charge; ongoing earnings exclude this one-time item.
Purchased expanded wildfire insurance coverage (~$40M annual premium NSP system; $300M Colorado layer per 10-K).
2026 ongoing earnings guidance set at $4.04-$4.16 per share on rate-base recovery and load growth.
Issued $5.4B common stock in 2025 (per equity roll-forward) to fund capex and maintain capital structure.
FY2024 dividends declared $2.19 per share; ongoing diluted EPS $3.50.
Accelerated renewables and transmission build-out across Minnesota, Colorado, Texas/New Mexico, and Wisconsin subsidiaries.
Marshall Fire ignited in Boulder County, Colorado (PSCo territory); litigation and settlement process began.

industry.structure: Xcel serves ~3.9 million electric and gas customers across eight states through four regulated utility subsidiaries (NSP-Minnesota, NSP-Wisconsin, PSCo, SPS). Returns are set by state and federal regulators on invested rate base, not market pricing.
industry.share_shift: Load growth is shifting toward large-load customers (AI data centers, industrial electrification) in Minnesota and Colorado; Xcel is winning interconnection agreements but must recover capital through rate cases and riders.
industry.trend: Utilities face simultaneous capex super-cycles (grid, renewables, transmission) and political affordability pressure; wildfire liability in Colorado and equity issuance to fund $60B of 2026-2030 capex are live capital-allocation themes.

We expect **7.84%** per year over 7 years at **~$79.84** on **$3.8/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Wildfire and climate-related liability in Colorado and the broader footprint, despite expanded insurance coverage purchased in 2025.
